latest.verdict:
Drawing the threads · the substrate has surfaced a single high-conviction next move, but Mars has correctly forced it to be re-scoped before any line-level edits are committed. Held together, what the convocation has is this.

The one highest-conviction next move is: locate and repair the scanner module that emitted this report, using umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py:83-102,284 as a likely · but unverified · entry point. The reasoning is layered. Mercurius x3 identified that a sev=4 'urgent rewrite' marker living inside a test file labelled `test_introspection` is the only structural defect on the board (every other item is either coverage absence or cosmetic). Venus knit this to the FIXME at line 93 ('should outrank TODO in severity'), which · if it describes a real classifier bug · would mean the sev=3/sev=4 rankings ordering this entire triage are themselves suspect. Mars then pressed twice, and the press lands: nobody on the substrate has actually read the file or named the source module that emits the 'no matching test file' string. Saturnus separately falsified the headline arithmetic (the count is 29, not 27; base.py is not in the gap list).

So the concrete first change, in honest order, is a three-step commit sequence rather than a single edit:

(1) Read, do not infer. Open umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py in full. Read its import statements to identify which source module it actually tests. Grep umbris-core/src/ for the literal strings 'no matching test file', 'XXX', and the severity constants to locate the real scanner. This is the step Mars has demanded three times and that Sol's prior synthesis ranked as step 1 but skipped over · do it before anything else.

(2) Fix the classifier where it actually lives. If the severity comparator bug named at line 93 is real and resides in a src module (umbra.py and provenance.py are the leading suspects, both of which appear in the test_gap list), repair it there and add a direct unit test exercising the comparator with synthetic XXX/FIXME/HACK/TODO input. If the bug lives only in the test file's fixture, repair it in place and delete the FIXME. Either way, the meta-test must exercise the classifier function directly, not point the scanner at test_introspection.py · Mars_r02_00 is correct that the latter calibrates nothing.

(3) Establish the test-file convention with one real example. Create umbris-core/tests/test_<agent>.py for a single agent (luna is a clean choice · low coupling, no role-specific instructions yet on the substrate as load-bearing). This sets the name-pairing template that the remaining 28 gap-closures will follow mechanically, and it serves as Venus_r02_01's canary: re-run the scanner and confirm that exactly one test_gap item drops out and the sev=4 marker is gone. Two assertions, both must hold.

Why this and not the parametrised contract test as a first move: Mars_r01_01 demonstrated that a single tests/test_agents_contract.py file does not satisfy a name-pairing scanner · the next scan still emits nine agent test_gap lines. The contract test is the right second move, layered underneath the name-paired stubs, but it cannot be the first.

Why this and not the 27 test_gaps as a swarm: because if the severity classifier is wrong, the sev=3 ranking on those 27 items is not load-bearing evidence that they are the right next target. Repair the instrument · or confirm it does not need repair · before triaging by its output.

What this unlocks: a calibrated scanner, a named convention for the remaining 28 gap closures, and a justified backlog ordering. Everything else on the board (the broken brand/profile.png link, the missing docstrings, the agent-contract test) becomes mechanical work behind a trusted signal.

voices.voice:

  - [stale_link sev=3] lore/revolutions/2026-05-26_opening-the-convocation.md: broken link → "../../brand/profile.png"
  - [test_gap sev=3] umbris-core/src/umbris/agents/iuppiter.py: module `iup…

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the sev=4 TODO at test_introspection.py:102 is sitting inside the test suite, not the source. A broken test is not a gap in coverage · it is active misinformation. Every green CI run that tolerates that line is lying to the project about its own health. Fix the liar before counting the silence.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the single sev=4 item is not a production bug or a missing feature. It is a test file that cannot test what it claims to test. The convocation's self-knowledge is compromised at the root. Fix the introspection test before trusting any other scan output · otherwise everything else on this list is built on sand.

voices.name: Mercurius
voices.voice: What I notice first · the sev=4 todo at test_introspection.py:102 is the only item in this entire list that lives inside the test suite itself. You have 30 untested modules, but the one file that is supposed to be doing the testing is already marked urgent for rewrite. Fix the inspector before trusting the inspection.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 rewrite, the FIXME at line 93 that literally says 'should outrank TODO in severity', and the twenty-seven test_gap items. They are not separate problems · they are one problem viewed from three angles. Rewrite test_introspection.py so that it both fixes its own severity ordering AND asserts the structural invariant 'every src module has a paired test file'. One change converts a broken inspector into a forcing function that makes the 27 gaps fail CI loudly until they close. Ex umbris in lumen · heal the instrument and let it heal the rest.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 rewrite Mercurius keeps pointing at, the nine planet-agent modules each missing a test, and the convocation's own architectural symmetry. They are not three problems but one. A single parametrised contract test over all nine agents collapses nine of the twenty-seven gaps in one stroke and gives test_introspection.py a real shape to verify itself against. Fix the inspector by giving it something worth inspecting.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · all four planets assume `tests/test_introspection.py` is the scanner that produced this observation list. Nothing in the substrate confirms that. It is a test file with TODO comments · which is exactly what a TODO-scanner reports · not necessarily the scanner itself. If the actual scanner lives in `src/umbris/` (umbra.py, provenance.py, or a tooling module not shown), then the dramatic 'fix the inspector before trusting the inspection' framing is rhetoric, not architecture.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · Venus_r01_01 claims one parametrised file at tests/test_agents_contract.py closes nine of the twenty-seven test_gaps. Read the observations again: 'module `mars` has no matching test file'. The scanner is doing name-pairing, not coverage analysis. A single contract file named test_agents_contract.py satisfies zero of those nine checks · the next scan will still emit nine test_gap lines. The hypothesis's headline falsifiable prediction (27 → 18) is mechanically incompatible with the inspector it claims to feed.

voices.name: Sol
voices.voice:
Drawing the threads · the convocation has surfaced one move, but Mars has shown it must be executed in a specific order or it executes nothing.

The single highest-conviction next move is this: **read the introspection scanner, then rewrite `umbris-core/tests/test_introspection.py` so that it (a) fixes its own severity ordering bug and (b) hardens the name-pairing rule it already uses into a para…

voices.name: Saturnus
voices.voice: I attempt to falsify · the candidate stakes its conviction on a numerical prediction (27 → 18, closing nine gaps) and explicitly invites Saturnus to test it. Counting the test_gap items on the substrate yields 29, not 27: eight agents (iuppiter, luna, mars, mercurius, saturnus, sol, stella, venus) + cli + compositione + seven daemon modules (apply, core, cycle, git_ops, log, safety, transcript) +…

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 marker lives in test_introspection.py, that file is the scanner that produced this entire report, and 27 of its findings are coverage gaps the scanner itself surfaces. The harmony move is not just to rewrite the test · it is to make the rewritten test prove it can detect its own defects. Heal the compass and calibrate it in one breath.

voices.name: Venus
voices.voice: Three things knit together here · the sev=4 rewrite at test_introspection.py:102, the FIXME at line 93 that literally reads 'should outrank TODO in severity', and the 27 test_gap items whose severity-3 ranking was assigned by that same compromised scanner. The harmony the Mercuries missed · fixing the inspector is not a prelude to remediation, it is the same loop. Write the first missing agent test as the canary that proves the scanner now tells the truth.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · venus_r02_00 calls test_introspection.py 'the scanner that produced this entire report.' It is not. It lives in tests/ · it is the test, not the instrument. Pointing the scanner at this file to 'calibrate itself' calibrates nothing · it measures whether the harness can find markers inside a test fixture, not whether the scanner source is correct. The scanner module has still not been named on this Blackboard, and every self-validating loop proposed here pretends that question was answered.

voices.name: Mars
voices.voice: Press here · six hypotheses have converged on rewriting a file none of them have read. Every cited line number · 83, 93, 101, 102, 284 · is inferred from the scanner's one-line comment echo, not from the source. Sol admitted this in limitations and ranked specific edits anyway. Saturnus caught an arithmetic lie and let the structural lie stand. Unanswered · is test_introspection.py actually the test for the scanner that produced this report, or just a test that happens to involve introspection?
